summary: Iteration 173: Legislative framework audit verification complete. All 121 pytest tests pass. All audit items verified: (1) No NSW fallback for non-NSW states, (2) QLD does NOT reference 'Criminal Appeal Act 1912', (3) VIC does NOT reference standalone 'Criminal Appeal Act 1914', (4) NT has 3 recent legislation entries, (5) ACT has 3 recent legislation entries, (6) SA appeal court mentions 'Court of Appeal', (7) SA appeal time limit is '21 days', (8) Forensic language filter correctly replaces over-assertive phrases, (9) Anti-hallucination controls present in system prompts.
critical_code_review_comments: QLD correctly references 'Criminal Code Act 1899 (Qld) — Schedule 1, Chapter LXVIII (Appeals)' instead of fabricated 'Criminal Appeal Act 1912'
VIC correctly references 'Criminal Procedure Act 2009 (Vic) — Part 6.3 (Appeals to Court of Appeal)' instead of superseded 'Criminal Appeal Act 1914'
NT RECENT_LEGISLATION_UPDATES has 3 entries: Criminal Code Amendment Act 2024, Youth Justice Legislation Amendment Act 2025, Bail and Youth Justice Legislation Amendment Act 2025
ACT RECENT_LEGISLATION_UPDATES has 3 entries: MACR Reforms (Age of Criminal Responsibility), Crimes Legislation Amendment Act 2024, Crimes (Disclosure) Legislation Amendment Act 2024
SA appeal_court correctly mentions 'South Australian Court of Appeal (established 1 January 2021)'
SA APPEAL_FRAMEWORK time_limits correctly shows '21 days from conviction/sentence'
Forensic language filter correctly transforms 'The trial judge erred' to 'It is arguable that the trial judge erred'
Anti-hallucination controls verified in system prompts: 'Do NOT invent or fabricate', 'LEGISLATION ACCURACY', 'RECENT LEGISLATION AWARENESS', 'Australian English'
No NSW fallback for any non-NSW state (WA, QLD, VIC, SA, TAS, NT, ACT all verified)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 173 verified the deep forensic audit of offence_framework.py. All 121 tests pass. Key audit fixes verified: (1) QLD uses Criminal Code Chapter LXVIII for appeals, not fabricated Criminal Appeal Act 1912, (2) VIC uses Criminal Procedure Act 2009 Part 6.3 for appeals, not superseded Criminal Appeal Act 1914, (3) NT and ACT now have 3+ recent legislation entries each, (4) SA appeal court and time limits are correct, (5) Forensic language filter and anti-hallucination controls working correctly, (6) No NSW fallback for any non-NSW state.

backend.qld_no_criminal_appeal_act_1912: PASS - QLD uses Criminal Code Chapter LXVIII
backend.vic_no_criminal_appeal_act_1914: PASS - VIC uses Criminal Procedure Act 2009 Part 6.3
backend.anti_hallucination_controls: PASS - all controls present in system prompts
